markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' multiple delays have pushed Grand Theft Auto VI to a confirmed November 19, 2026 release, creating a roughly six-month window from now that significantly raises the odds of major external events occurring beforehand. Take-Two's repeated postponements—from an initial 2025 target to May and then November 2026—reflect the studio's emphasis on polish for its flagship open-world title, extending the period for unrelated developments in news, music, and pop culture to resolve. Traders are closely watching upcoming catalysts such as the next earnings call for any status updates, potential new trailers, and high-profile releases or geopolitical shifts that could influence market-implied probabilities on specific outcomes. This extended timeline amplifies uncertainty around what qualifies as "before" launch, favoring outcomes with strong momentum in the interim.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and parent company Take-Two Interactive have officially delayed Grand Theft Auto VI twice since its initial 2025 target, settling on a November 19, 2026 release date after shifting from May 26, 2026. Recent fiscal earnings calls and forward-looking product schedules list the title firmly for that November window on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, with no revisions signaled and development described as on track for additional polish. This established timeline, backed by the publisher’s investor disclosures just weeks before the June 2026 cutoff, drives the overwhelming trader consensus reflected in the 99.9% implied probability for “No.” While an unforeseen acceleration or surprise early launch remains theoretically possible in the high-stakes entertainment industry, historical patterns of Rockstar’s deliberate pacing and the absence of any contradictory signals make such an upset highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape trial has sharply tilted trader sentiment toward no additional prison time.** Jury deadlock on May 15, 2026, marked the second hung jury in a year on the Jessica Mann allegations, leaving prosecutors to weigh whether pursuing a fourth trial is viable amid mounting procedural fatigue and defense claims of juror bias. At 73 and reportedly battling serious health issues including recent heart surgery, Weinstein has already served more than six years at Rikers while his separate 16-year Los Angeles sentence runs consecutively. The lower-level rape charge carries a maximum of just four years—well below time served—while any potential sentencing on the earlier mixed-verdict sexual-assault conviction could be offset by credit for prior incarceration. These factors, combined with the case’s long history of appeals and retrials, underpin the market’s 75.9% implied probability for no further prison time, with traders viewing prolonged additional incarceration as increasingly unlikely before the next procedural milestones.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s position as General Secretary and Central Military Commission chairman remains highly consolidated ahead of the 21st Party Congress in 2027, driving the 92.8% trader consensus that he will not be removed before the end of 2026. Recent actions, including the October 2025 Fourth Plenum’s personnel purges focused on military leadership alongside adoption of recommendations for the 2026–2030 Five-Year Plan, underscore policy continuity and centralized authority rather than transition pressures. Ongoing diplomatic engagements and emphasis on long-term economic and security priorities in official addresses further signal stability, with no public indicators of elite opposition, health issues, or institutional challenges capable of altering the outcome within the market’s timeframe.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2028 Democratic presidential nomination features a fragmented field in which no candidate has yet consolidated broad support. Gavin Newsom leads early trader pricing through his California governorship and national fundraising network, followed by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez drawing progressive activist backing and Kamala Harris retaining residual name recognition from her prior vice-presidential and 2024 campaign roles. Other governors and senators such as Josh Shapiro, Jon Ossoff, and Pete Buttigieg occupy mid-tier positions based on state-level records or congressional visibility. Key variables that could shift probabilities include primary polling averages in early states, major endorsements, midterm performance, and the emergence of a clear fundraising or organizational frontrunner ahead of formal delegate selection.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2028 Democratic presidential nomination features a fragmented field in which no candidate has yet consolidated broad support. Gavin Newsom leads early trader pricing through his California governorship and national fundraising network, followed by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez drawing progressive activist backing and Kamala Harris retaining residual name recognition from her prior vice-presidential and 2024 campaign roles. Other governors and senators such as Josh Shapiro, Jon Ossoff, and Pete Buttigieg occupy mid-tier positions based on state-level records or congressional visibility. Key variables that could shift probabilities include primary polling averages in early states, major endorsements, midterm performance, and the emergence of a clear fundraising or organizational frontrunner ahead of formal delegate selection.

Other governors and senators such as Josh Shapiro, Jon Ossoff, and Pete Buttigieg occupy mid-tier positions based on state-level records or congressional visibility. Key variables that could shift probabilities include primary polling averages in early states, major endorsements, midterm performance, and the emergence of a clear fundraising or organizational frontrunner ahead of formal delegate selection.

markets.question: Will Phil Murphy win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.
